seoTitle: Gravesend vs Grays Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gravesend and Grays Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Gravesend vs Grays Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gravesend and Grays Point.
heading: Gravesend vs Grays Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gravesend, New South Wales and Grays Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Grays Point has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Gravesend (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gravesend or Grays Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gravesend has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Grays Point scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gravesend scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Grays Point scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gravesend or Grays Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Grays Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
